problem_text_cn: 二、（18分）循环过程效率。（1）单原子分子理想气体所经历的两个热循环过程ABCA和ACDA如图1所示，试求它们的效率之比$\eta_{ABCA}:\eta_{ACDA}$。（2）图2中所示为某理想气体可取的三个热循环过程ABCA，ACDA和ABCDA，各自的效率依次记为$\eta_1$，$\eta_2$和$\eta_3$。已知$\eta_1$，试求$\eta_2$和$\eta_3$，再为$\eta_1$，$\eta_2$和$\eta_3$的大小排序。
problem_text_en: 2. (18 points) Efficiency of cyclic processes. (1) A monatomic ideal gas undergoes two thermal cycles ABCA and ACDA as shown in Figure 1. Find the ratio of their efficiencies $\eta_{ABCA}:\eta_{ACDA}$. (2) Figure 2 shows three thermal cycles ABCA, ACDA, and ABCDA available for an ideal gas, with their efficiencies denoted as $\eta_1$, $\eta_2$, and $\eta_3$ respectively. Given $\eta_1$, find $\eta_2$ and $\eta_3$, and then sort $\eta_1$, $\eta_2$, and $\eta_3$ by magnitude.
img_cn: 图833_1：p-V图中循环过程ABCA和ACDA示意图
图833_2：p-V图中循环过程ABCA、ACDA和ABCDA示意图
img_en: Fig. 833_1: Schematic of cyclic processes ABCA and ACDA in p-V diagram
Fig. 833_2: Schematic of cyclic processes ABCA, ACDA and ABCDA in p-V diagram
problem_solution_cn:
【分析与解】
（1）循环效率$\eta=\dfrac{W}{Q_{吸}}$，两个循环做功相等（$W_{ABCA}=W_{ACDA}$），故效率之比等于吸热的反比：$\eta_{ABCA}:\eta_{ACDA}=Q_{ACDA}:Q_{ABCA}$。
单原子分子理想气体的定容摩尔热容$C_{V,\text{m}}=\dfrac{3}{2}R$，定压摩尔热容$C_{p,\text{m}}=\dfrac{5}{2}R$；由理想气体状态方程得$T_B=2T_A$，$T_C=4T_A$。
- 循环ABCA的吸热：$Q_{ABCA}=\nu C_{V,\text{m}}(T_B-T_A)+\nu C_{p,\text{m}}(T_C-T_B)=\dfrac{13}{2}\nu RT_A$；
- 循环ACDA的吸热：$Q_{ACDA}=\dfrac{3}{2}p_0V_0+\nu C_{V,\text{m}}(T_C-T_A)=6\nu RT_A$。
因此效率之比：$\eta_{ABCA}:\eta_{ACDA}=12:13$。

（2）设$Q_{ABC吸}=Q_1$，$Q_{CA放}=Q_2$，$Q_{CDA放}=Q_3$，三个循环做功满足$W_{ABCA}=W_{ACDA}=W$，$W_{ABCDA}=2W$。
- 由$\eta_1=\dfrac{W}{Q_1}$，得$\eta_3=\dfrac{2W}{Q_1}=2\eta_1$；
- 由效率定义$\eta=1-\dfrac{Q_{放}}{Q_{吸}}$，得$1-\eta_3=(1-\eta_1)(1-\eta_2)$，代入$\eta_3=2\eta_1$解得：$\eta_2=\dfrac{\eta_1}{1-\eta_1}$。
效率大小排序：由$\eta_2=\dfrac{\eta_1}{1-\eta_1}>\eta_1$，$\eta_3=\dfrac{2\eta_2}{1+\eta_2}>\eta_2$，得$\boldsymbol{\eta_3>\eta_2>\eta_1}$。
problem_solution_en:
[Analysis and Solution]
(1) The efficiency of a cycle is $\eta=\dfrac{W}{Q_{absorbed}}$. The two cycles do equal work ($W_{ABCA}=W_{ACDA}$), so the ratio of efficiencies is the inverse ratio of absorbed heat: $\eta_{ABCA}:\eta_{ACDA}=Q_{ACDA}:Q_{ABCA}$. 
For a monatomic ideal gas, the molar heat capacity at constant volume $C_{V,\text{m}}=\dfrac{3}{2}R$, and the molar heat capacity at constant pressure $C_{p,\text{m}}=\dfrac{5}{2}R$. From the ideal gas state equation, $T_B=2T_A$ and $T_C=4T_A$.
- Absorbed heat for cycle ABCA: $Q_{ABCA}=\nu C_{V,\text{m}}(T_B-T_A)+\nu C_{p,\text{m}}(T_C-T_B)=\dfrac{13}{2}\nu RT_A$;
- Absorbed heat for cycle ACDA: $Q_{ACDA}=\dfrac{3}{2}p_0V_0+\nu C_{V,\text{m}}(T_C-T_A)=6\nu RT_A$.
Thus, the ratio of efficiencies is $\eta_{ABCA}:\eta_{ACDA}=12:13$.

(2) Let $Q_{ABC absorbed}=Q_1$, $Q_{CA released}=Q_2$, $Q_{CDA released}=Q_3$. The work done by the three cycles satisfies $W_{ABCA}=W_{ACDA}=W$ and $W_{ABCDA}=2W$.
- From $\eta_1=\dfrac{W}{Q_1}$, we get $\eta_3=\dfrac{2W}{Q_1}=2\eta_1$;
- From the efficiency definition $\eta=1-\dfrac{Q_{released}}{Q_{absorbed}}$, we have $1-\eta_3=(1-\eta_1)(1-\eta_2)$. Substituting $\eta_3=2\eta_1$ gives $\eta_2=\dfrac{\eta_1}{1-\eta_1}$.
Sorting of efficiencies: Since $\eta_2=\dfrac{\eta_1}{1-\eta_1}>\eta_1$ and $\eta_3=\dfrac{2\eta_2}{1+\eta_2}>\eta_2$, we obtain $\boldsymbol{\eta_3>\eta_2>\eta_1}$.
